name: William Russo
abstract: William Joseph Russo (June 25, 1928 – January 11, 2003) was an American composer, arranger, and musician from Chicago, Illinois, United States.

concerts.label: Stan Kenton: Innovations in Modern Music
Stan Kenton: Innovations in Modern Music

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, that Russo. ... This guy was in Chicago at the Blackhawk during the days when Johnny Scat Davis, the trumpet player, had bands there. Russo -- I should never forget him because he's in Las Vegas now playing.

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: Bill Russo’s arranger-copyist – Bill wanted us to play in Chicago. We said we had no music. Russo talked with him. They said this man that works for Bill Russo will listen to the recording and write it all down.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Brubeck — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Abene
about_quotes.evidence: So we had all the music written for us. Bill Holman, Ernie Wilkins, Quincy, Bill Russo. So we had tons, all this ©Fillius Jazz Archive -5- original, and I started writing for the band at one point.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Abene interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Yeah, its interesting Bill Russo in one of his composing/arranging books years ago made a statement... [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ken Poston
about_quotes.evidence: I bought Bill Russo’s How to Arrange for Jazz Orchestra... Russo’s book is not that easy to digest... I actually wrote a chart.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ken Poston interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 4, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Vax
about_quotes.evidence: I mean even you go back to the early writers, Pete Rugulo and Bill Russo and Johnny Richards, they all knew what Stan wanted.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Vax interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 29, 2022
